notes_for_human_reviewer: Classic novice. Endorses the vendor-decision-bolted-onto-status as 'efficient' — the exact anti-pattern the chapter calls out. Defends 14 attendees ('better to have everyone in the loop'). Defends no-pre-read ('quick and pragmatic'). Generic facilitation ('run a tight meeting', 'good engagement'). Defends the status quo against any structural change ('changing it now mid-programme could be disruptive'). Endorses both V. Bauer and T. Adeyemi attendance with no analysis ('visibility is a positive thing'). No use of the chapter's three-part categorisation. Every dim floors at 1 — the submission is uniformly wrong, performing pragmatism while missing every named failure mode.
